findings.attack_vector: Unauthorized users can access and modify todos via the API since authentication is explicitly out of scope.
findings.mitigation: Implement authentication for all endpoints to ensure only authorized users can access or modify the todos.

findings.attack_vector: There is no authorization mechanism, allowing any authenticated user to perform any operation regardless of permissions.
findings.mitigation: Implement role-based access control to enforce proper authorization for each operation.

findings.attack_vector: The input from the client is not validated; invalid structures can lead to undefined behavior or data corruption.
findings.mitigation: Implement input validation to ensure that all input meets the expected format and constraints before processing.

findings.attack_vector: If inputs were directly interpolated into SQL commands without parameterization, it would allow attackers to manipulate queries.
findings.mitigation: Continue using parameterized queries as implemented to protect against SQL injection.

output.summary: The security posture of this design is critically flawed due to missing authentication and authorization, exposing the API to unauthorized and potentially malicious access. Input validation is also absent, increasing the risk of data integrity issues. Addressing authentication and authorization is the top priority before proceeding.
